items.title: Tesla claims driver ‘manually overrode self-driving’ in deadly Texas crash
items.topic: Tesla claims driver ‘manually overrode self-driving’ in deadly Texas crash
items.summary: Tesla claims that the driver manually overrode the self-driving system in a fatal crash in Texas. This incident raises questions about the reliability and safety of Tesla's autonomous driving technology, particularly in scenarios where human intervention is required.
items.abstract: Tesla claims that the driver manually overrode the self-driving system in a fatal crash in Texas. This incident raises questions about the reliability and safety of Tesla's autonomous driving technology, particularly in scenarios where human intervention is required.

items.title: EXCLUSIVE: Sweden may oppose Tesla's supervised self-driving tech in Europe over speeding concerns
items.topic: EXCLUSIVE: Sweden may oppose Tesla's supervised self-driving tech in Europe over speeding concerns
items.summary: Sweden may oppose Tesla's supervised self-driving technology in Europe due to concerns over speeding. This regulatory stance could impact Tesla's deployment of autonomous features in the European market, highlighting the ongoing tension between technological advancement and safety standards.
items.abstract: Sweden may oppose Tesla's supervised self-driving technology in Europe due to concerns over speeding. This regulatory stance could impact Tesla's deployment of autonomous features in the European market, highlighting the ongoing tension between technological advancement and safety standards.

items.title: A new, inexpensive Chinese AI model is catching up with Anthropic, OpenAI on their home turf
items.topic: A new, inexpensive Chinese AI model is catching up with Anthropic, OpenAI on their home turf
items.summary: A new, inexpensive Chinese AI model is rapidly catching up with Anthropic and OpenAI in performance. This development underscores the growing competitiveness of Chinese AI research and the potential for more affordable AI solutions, which could have implications for autonomous vehicle development.

items.title: Portugal launches first open-source AI model, joining Europe's sovereignty push
items.topic: Portugal launches first open-source AI model, joining Europe's sovereignty push
items.summary: Portugal has launched its first open-source AI model, joining Europe's push for AI sovereignty. This initiative aims to reduce dependency on foreign AI technologies and enhance local innovation, which could also benefit research in autonomous vehicles.
items.abstract: Portugal has launched its first open-source AI model, joining Europe's push for AI sovereignty. This initiative aims to reduce dependency on foreign AI technologies and enhance local innovation, which could also benefit research in autonomous vehicles.

items.title: China's Meituan says new AI model trained on domestic chips
items.topic: China's Meituan says new AI model trained on domestic chips
items.summary: China's Meituan has developed a new AI model trained on domestic chips, demonstrating the country's growing capability in AI hardware and software. This could lead to more localized and potentially cost-effective solutions for autonomous vehicle systems.
items.abstract: China's Meituan has developed a new AI model trained on domestic chips, demonstrating the country's growing capability in AI hardware and software. This could lead to more localized and potentially cost-effective solutions for autonomous vehicle systems.

items.title: OpenAI staggers AI model release after Trump administration request
items.topic: OpenAI staggers AI model release after Trump administration request
items.summary: OpenAI has delayed the release of a new AI model following a request from the Trump administration. This delay highlights the increasing regulatory scrutiny of AI technologies, which could have broader implications for the development and deployment of autonomous vehicles.
items.abstract: OpenAI has delayed the release of a new AI model following a request from the Trump administration. This delay highlights the increasing regulatory scrutiny of AI technologies, which could have broader implications for the development and deployment of autonomous vehicles.

items.title: Generative artificial intelligence creates delicious, sustainable, and nutritious burgers | npj Science of Food
items.topic: Generative artificial intelligence creates delicious, sustainable, and nutritious burgers | npj Science of Food
items.summary: A study published in npj Science of Food demonstrates the use of generative AI to create sustainable, nutritious, and delicious burgers. While not directly related to autonomous vehicles, this application of AI in food technology showcases the versatility and potential of generative models in various industries.

While not directly related to autonomous vehicles, this application of AI in food technology showcases the versatility and potential of generative models in various industries.

items.title: Handling the Hidden: Occlusion-Aware Motion Prediction for Autonomous Vehicles
items.topic: Handling the Hidden: Occlusion-Aware Motion Prediction for Autonomous Vehicles
items.source: IEEE Open Journal of Intelligent Transportation Systems
items.summary: The IEEE Open Journal of Intelligent Transportation Systems published a paper on Handling the Hidden: Occlusion-Aware Motion Prediction for Autonomous Vehicles on March 25, 2026, which extends the MultIAMP framework to handle occlusions using real perception inputs, enabling safer navigation in dynamic environments. This approach systematically detects, processes, and anticipates the behavior of occluded areas. The system is quantitatively evaluated using the CARLA simulator. The proposed method improves situational awareness and reduces safety risks in autonomous vehicle deployment.
items.abstract: The IEEE Open Journal of Intelligent Transportation Systems published a paper on Handling the Hidden: Occlusion-Aware Motion Prediction for Autonomous Vehicles on March 25, 2026, which extends the MultIAMP framework to handle occlusions using real perception inputs, enabling safer navigation in dynamic environments. This approach systematically detects, processes, and anticipates the behavior of occluded areas. The system is quantitatively evaluated using the CARLA simulator. The proposed method improves situational awareness and reduces safety risks in autonomous vehicle deployment.

items.title: Evolution of Deep Learning Techniques and Challenges for Vehicle Trajectory Prediction
items.topic: Evolution of Deep Learning Techniques and Challenges for Vehicle Trajectory Prediction
items.source: IEEE Open Journal of Intelligent Transportation Systems
items.summary: The IEEE Open Journal of Intelligent Transportation Systems published a paper on Evolution of Deep Learning Techniques and Challenges for Vehicle Trajectory Prediction on May 15, 2026, which provides a comprehensive review of recent deep learning-based approaches for vehicle trajectory prediction from a system and modeling perspective. The paper introduces a system-oriented taxonomy of vehicle trajectory prediction methods, including classical, reinforcement learning-based, collaborative, and deep learning-based methods. This review aims to bridge the gap in existing surveys and provide insights into emerging trends in model design. The paper's findings are relevant to the advancement of intelligent transportation systems and autonomous driving.
items.abstract: The IEEE Open Journal of Intelligent Transportation Systems published a paper on Evolution of Deep Learning Techniques and Challenges for Vehicle Trajectory Prediction on May 15, 2026, which provides a comprehensive review of recent deep learning-based approaches for vehicle trajectory prediction from a system and modeling perspective. The paper introduces a system-oriented taxonomy of vehicle trajectory prediction methods, including classical, reinforcement learning-based, collaborative, and deep learning-based methods. This review aims to bridge the gap in existing surveys and provide insights into emerging trends in model design. The paper's findings are relevant to the advancement of intelligent transportation systems and autonomous driving.

items.title: Socially Compatible and Risk-Aware Driving: A Cognition-Inspired Interactive Decision-Making Framework for Autonomous Vehicles
items.topic: Socially Compatible and Risk-Aware Driving: A Cognition-Inspired Interactive Decision-Making Framework for Autonomous Vehicles
items.source: IEEE Open Journal of Intelligent Transportation Systems
items.summary: The IEEE Open Journal of Intelligent Transportation Systems published a paper on Socially Compatible and Risk-Aware Driving: A Cognition-Inspired Interactive Decision-Making Framework for Autonomous Vehicles on March 24, 2026, which proposes a cognitively and socially grounded interactive decision-making framework for autonomous vehicles to address the challenges of risk-aware and socially compatible behaviors in complex mixed traffic environments. The architecture integrates a Traffic Situation Risk Field (TSRF) module, which assesses environmental risks from a social-cognitive perspective. This framework enables autonomous vehicles to exhibit risk-aware and socially compatible behaviors, ensuring safety and efficiency in interactions with human-driven vehicles.
items.abstract: The IEEE Open Journal of Intelligent Transportation Systems published a paper on Socially Compatible and Risk-Aware Driving: A Cognition-Inspired Interactive Decision-Making Framework for Autonomous Vehicles on March 24, 2026, which proposes a cognitively and socially grounded interactive decision-making framework for autonomous vehicles to address the challenges of risk-aware and socially compatible behaviors in complex mixed traffic environments. The architecture integrates a Traffic Situation Risk Field (TSRF) module, which assesses environmental risks from a social-cognitive perspective. This framework enables autonomous vehicles to exhibit risk-aware and socially compatible behaviors, ensuring safety and efficiency in interactions with human-driven vehicles.
